input.prompt:
I upgraded my test project from MSTest.Sdk/3.8.0 to MSTest.Sdk/4.1.0 and now I get
these compilation errors:
- CS1061: 'TestContext' does not contain a definition for 'ManagedType'
- CS0103: The name 'TestTimeout' does not exist in the current context
- CS1061: 'IDictionary<string, object>' does not contain a definition for 'Contains'
How do I fix these errors? Also, is there anything else I should know about
MSTest.Sdk v4 behavior changes?

Contents of https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/core/testing/unit-testing-mstest-sdk:
This article covers advanced configuration options for MSTest.Sdk. For basic setup and getting started, see [Get started with MSTest](unit-testing-mstest-getting-started).

Important

By default, MSTest.Sdk uses the [MSTest runner with MTP](unit-testing-mstest-running-tests), including with [dotnet test](unit-testing-with-dotnet-test). This requires modifying your CI and local CLI calls, and also impacts the available entries of the _.runsettings_. You can keep the old integrations and tools by [switching to VSTest](#select-the-runner).

MSTest.Sdk sets `EnableMSTestRunner` and `TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport` to true by default. For more information about dotnet test and its different modes, see [Testing with dotnet test](unit-testing-with-dotnet-test).

## Test utility helper libraries

If the project that uses MSTest.Sdk is intended to be a test utility helper library, and doesn't by itself contain any runnable tests, the project should have `<IsTestApplication>false</IsTestApplication>`.

## Select the runner

By default, MSTest SDK relies on [MTP](unit-testing-mstest-running-tests), but you can switch to [VSTest](/en-us/visualstudio/test/vstest-console-options) by adding the property `<UseVSTest>true</UseVSTest>`.

## Extend MTP

You can customize the MTP experience through a set of [NuGet package extensions](microsoft-testing-platform-features). To simplify and improve this experience, MSTest SDK introduces two features:

*   [Microsoft.Testing.Platform profile](#microsofttestingplatform-profile)
*   [Enable or disable extensions](#enable-or-disable-extensions)

### Microsoft.Testing.Platform profile

The concept of _profiles_ allows you to select the default set of configurations and extensions that will be applied to your test project.

You can set the profile using the property `TestingExtensionsProfile` with one of the following three profiles:

*   `None` - No extensions are enabled.
    
*   `Default` - Enables the recommended extensions for this version of MSTest.SDK. This is the default when the property isn't set explicitly.
    
    Enables the following extensions:
    
    *   [Code Coverage](microsoft-testing-platform-code-coverage#microsoft-code-coverage)
    *   [Trx Report](microsoft-testing-platform-test-reports#visual-studio-test-reports-trx)
*   `AllMicrosoft` - Enable all extensions shipped by Microsoft (including extensions with a restrictive license).
    
    Enables the following extensions:
    
    *   [Code Coverage](microsoft-testing-platform-code-coverage#microsoft-code-coverage)
    *   [Crash Dump](microsoft-testing-platform-crash-hang-dumps#crash-dump)
    *   [Fakes](microsoft-testing-platform-fakes) (MSTest.Sdk 3.7.0+)
    *   [Hang Dump](microsoft-testing-platform-crash-hang-dumps#hang-dump)
    *   [Hot Reload](microsoft-testing-platform-hot-reload#hot-reload)
    *   [Retry](microsoft-testing-platform-retry#retry)
    *   [Trx Report](microsoft-testing-platform-test-reports#visual-studio-test-reports-trx)
    *   [AzureDevOpsReport](microsoft-testing-platform-test-reports#azure-devops-reports)

Here's a full example, using the `None` profile:

```
<Project Sdk="MSTest.Sdk/4.1.0">

    <PropertyGroup>
        <TargetFramework>net10.0</TargetFramework>
        <TestingExtensionsProfile>None</TestingExtensionsProfile>
    </PropertyGroup>

</Project>
```

Extension/Profile

None

Default

AllMicrosoft

[Code Coverage](https://www.nuget.org/packages/Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.CodeCoverage)

✔️

✔️

[Crash Dump](https://www.nuget.org/packages/Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.CrashDump)

✔️

[Fakes](https://www.nuget.org/packages/Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.Fakes)

✔️¹

[Hang Dump](https://www.nuget.org/packages/Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.HangDump)

✔️

[Hot Reload](https://www.nuget.org/packages/Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.HotReload)

✔️

[Retry](https://www.nuget.org/packages/Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.Retry)

✔️

[Trx](https://www.nuget.org/packages/Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.TrxReport)

✔️

✔️

[AzureDevOpsReport](microsoft-testing-platform-test-reports#azure-devops-reports)

✔️²

¹ MSTest.Sdk 3.7.0+ ² MSTest.Sdk 3.11.0+

### Enable or disable extensions

Extensions can be enabled and disabled by MSBuild properties with the pattern `Enable[NugetPackageNameWithoutDots]`.

For example, to enable the crash dump extension (NuGet package [Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.CrashDump](https://www.nuget.org/packages/Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.CrashDump)), you can use the following property `EnableMicrosoftTestingExtensionsCrashDump` set to `true`:

```
<Project Sdk="MSTest.Sdk/4.1.0">

<PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net10.0</TargetFramework>
    <EnableMicrosoftTestingExtensionsCrashDump>true</EnableMicrosoftTestingExtensionsCrashDump>
</PropertyGroup>

</Project>
```

For a list of all available extensions, see [MTP features](microsoft-testing-platform-features).

Warning

It's important to review the licensing terms for each extension as they might vary.

Enabled and disabled extensions are combined with the extensions provided by your selected extension profile.

This property pattern can be used to enable an additional extension on top of the implicit `Default` profile (as seen in the previous CrashDumpExtension example).

You can also disable an extension that's coming from the selected profile. For example, disable the `MS Code Coverage` extension by setting `<EnableMicrosoftTestingExtensionsCodeCoverage>false</EnableMicrosoftTestingExtensionsCodeCoverage>`:

```
<Project Sdk="MSTest.Sdk/4.1.0">

    <PropertyGroup>
        <TargetFramework>net10.0</TargetFramework>
        <EnableMicrosoftTestingExtensionsCodeCoverage>false</EnableMicrosoftTestingExtensionsCodeCoverage>
    </PropertyGroup>

</Project>
```

## Features

Outside of the selection of the runner and runner-specific extensions, `MSTest.Sdk` also provides additional features to simplify and enhance your testing experience.

### Test with Aspire

Aspire is an opinionated, cloud-ready stack for building observable, production ready, distributed applications. Aspire is delivered through a collection of NuGet packages that handle specific cloud-native concerns. For more information, see the [Aspire docs](/en-us/dotnet/aspire/get-started/aspire-overview).

Note

This feature is available from MSTest.Sdk 3.4.0.

By setting the property `EnableAspireTesting` to `true`, you can bring all dependencies and default `using` directives you need for testing with `Aspire` and `MSTest`.

```
<Project Sdk="MSTest.Sdk/4.1.0">

    <PropertyGroup>
        <TargetFramework>net10.0</TargetFramework>
        <EnableAspireTesting>true</EnableAspireTesting>
    </PropertyGroup>

</Project>
```

### Test with Playwright

Playwright enables reliable end-to-end testing for modern web apps. For more information, see the official [Playwright docs](https://playwright.dev/dotnet/docs/intro).

Note

This feature is available from MSTest.Sdk 3.4.0.

By setting the property `EnablePlaywright` to `true` you can bring in all the dependencies and default `using` directives you need for testing with `Playwright` and `MSTest`.

```
<Project Sdk="MSTest.Sdk/4.1.0">

    <PropertyGroup>
        <TargetFramework>net10.0</TargetFramework>
        <EnablePlaywright>true</EnablePlaywright>
    </PropertyGroup>

</Project>
```

## Migrate to MSTest SDK

Consider the following steps that are required to migrate to the MSTest SDK.

### Update your project

When migrating an existing MSTest test project to MSTest SDK, start by replacing the `Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk"` entry at the top of your test project with `Sdk="MSTest.Sdk"`

```
- Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk"
+ Sdk="MSTest.Sdk"
```

Add the version to your `global.json`:

```
{
    "msbuild-sdks": {
        "MSTest.Sdk": "4.1.0"
    }
}
```

You can then start simplifying your project.

Remove default properties:

```
- <EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
- <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>
- <IsPackable>false</IsPackable>
- <IsTestProject>true</IsTestProject>
```

Remove default package references:

```
- <PackageReference Include="MSTest"
- <PackageReference Include="MSTest.TestFramework"
- <PackageReference Include="MSTest.TestAdapter"
- <PackageReference Include="MSTest.Analyzers"
- <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk"
```

Finally, based on the extensions profile you're using, you can also remove some of the `Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.*` packages.

### Update your CI

Once you've updated your projects, if you're using MTP (default) and if you rely on `dotnet test` to run your tests, you must update your CI configuration. For more information and to guide your understanding of all the required changes, see [dotnet test integration](unit-testing-with-dotnet-test).

If you're using the VSTest mode of `dotnet test`, here's an example update when using the `DotNetCoreCLI` task in Azure DevOps:

```
\- task: DotNetCoreCLI@2
  inputs:
    command: 'test'
    projects: '**/**.sln'
-    arguments: '--configuration Release'
+    arguments: '--configuration Release -- --report-trx --results-directory $(Agent.TempDirectory) --coverage'
```

## Known limitations

The NuGet-provided MSBuild SDKs (including MSTest.Sdk) have [limited tooling support](https://github.com/NuGet/Home/issues/13127) when it comes to updating their version, meaning that the usual NuGet update and Visual Studio UI for managing NuGet packages doesn't work as expected. You'll need to manually update the version in the `global.json` file and in the project file. (This applies even if you use Dependabot due to issues [dependabot-core#12824](https://github.com/dependabot/dependabot-core/issues/12824) and [dependabot-core#8615](https://github.com/dependabot/dependabot-core/issues/8615).)

## See also

*   [MTP–related properties](../project-sdk/msbuild-props#microsofttestingplatformrelated-properties)
*   [VSTest–related properties](../project-sdk/msbuild-props#vstestrelated-properties)


## Migrate to MSTest SDK

Consider the following steps that are required to migrate to the MSTest SDK.

### Update your project

When migrating an existing MSTest test project to MSTest SDK, start by replacing the `Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk"` entry at the top of your test project with `Sdk="MSTest.Sdk"`

```
- Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk"
+ Sdk="MSTest.Sdk"
```

Add the version to your `global.json`:

```
{
    "msbuild-sdks": {
        "MSTest.Sdk": "4.1.0"
    }
}
```

You can then start simplifying your project.

Remove default properties:

```
- <EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
- <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>
- <IsPackable>false</IsPackable>
- <IsTestProject>true</IsTestProject>
```

Remove default package references:

```
- <PackageReference Include="MSTest"
- <PackageReference Include="MSTest.TestFramework"
- <PackageReference Include="MSTest.TestAdapter"
- <PackageReference Include="MSTest.Analyzers"
- <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk"
```

Finally, based on the extensions profile you're using, you can also remove some of the `Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.*` packages.

### Update your CI

Once you've updated your projects, if you're using MTP (default) and if you rely on `dotnet test` to run your tests, you must update your CI configuration. For more information and to guide your understanding of all the required changes, see [dotnet test integration](unit-testing-with-dotnet-test).

If you're using the VSTest mode of `dotnet test`, here's an example update when using the `DotNetCoreCLI` task in Azure DevOps:

```
\- task: DotNetCoreCLI@2
  inputs:
    command: 'test'
    projects: '**/**.sln'
-    arguments: '--configuration Release'
+    arguments: '--configuration Release -- --report-trx --results-directory $(Agent.TempDirectory) --coverage'
```

## Known limitations

The NuGet-provided MSBuild SDKs (including MSTest.Sdk) have [limited tooling support](https://github.com/NuGet/Home/issues/13127) when it comes to updating their version, meaning that the usual NuGet update and Visual Studio UI for managing NuGet packages doesn't work as expected. You'll need to manually update the version in the `global.json` file and in the project file. (This applies even if you use Dependabot due to issues [dependabot-core#12824](https://github.com/dependabot/dependabot-core/issues/12824) and [dependabot-core#8615](https://github.com/dependabot/dependabot-core/issues/8615).)

## See also

*   [MTP–related properties](../project-sdk/msbuild-props#microsofttestingplatformrelated-properties)
*   [VSTest–related properties](../project-sdk/msbuild-props#vstestrelated-properties)

Tip

Starting with MSTest.TestFramework 3.7, the MSTest.Analyzers NuGet package is a dependency of the framework. For earlier versions, you need to use `MSTest` metapackage or add a package reference for `MSTest.Analyzers` explicitly.

## MSTestAnalysisMode

Starting with MSTest 3.8, an MSBuild property named `MSTestAnalysisMode` is available to determine which analyzers are enabled at which severity.

Tip

To see which rules are enabled at which severity for each mode, you can navigate to the package of the version of interest in NuGet cache, locate the `globalconfigs` directory, and open the `.globalconfig` file corresponding to the analysis mode. For more information on finding the NuGet cache directory, see [Managing the global packages, cache, and temp folders](/en-us/nuget/consume-packages/managing-the-global-packages-and-cache-folders). From that directory, locate `mstest.analyzers` directory, then the version (3.8 and higher), then `globalconfigs`. Alternatively, you can download the NuGet package of the version of interest from `nuget.org` and view it in NuGet Package Explorer (Windows app), or view directly in the [web app version of NuGet Package Explorer](https://nuget.info/packages/MSTest.Analyzers/).

The available values for this property:

*   [`None`](#none)
*   [`Default`](#default)
*   [`Recommended`](#recommended)
*   [`All`](#all)

### `None`

This value sets all analyzers to `none` severity, disabling all of them. You can then enable individual analyzers using `.editorconfig` or `.globalconfig` files.

### `Default`

This setting follows the default documented behavior for each rule.

*   Rules that are enabled by default will use their default severity.
*   Rules that are disabled by default will use `none` severity.

Note

Rules that are enabled by default as warnings are violations that are expected to cause issues at runtime.

### `Recommended`

This is the mode most developers are expected to use. Rules that are enabled by default with Info (`suggestion`) severity are escalated to warnings. The following rules are escalated to errors in both `Recommended` and `All` modes:

*   [MSTEST0003: Test methods should have valid layout](mstest0003).
*   [MSTEST0043: Use retry attribute on test method](mstest0043).

### `All`

This mode is more aggressive than `Recommended`. All rules are enabled as warnings. In addition, the following rules are escalated to errors:

*   [MSTEST0003: Test methods should have valid layout](mstest0003).
*   [MSTEST0043: Use retry attribute on test method](mstest0043).

## Rules by category

The analyzer rules are organized into the following categories:

### Design rules

[Design rules](design-rules) help you create and maintain test suites that adhere to proper design and good practices.

### Performance rules

[Performance rules](performance-rules) support high-performance testing.

### Suppression rules

[Suppression rules](suppression-rules) support suppressing diagnostics from other rules.

### Usage rules

[Usage rules](usage-rules) support proper usage of MSTest.

## Rules by concept

Find rules organized by common testing scenarios and concepts:

### Test structure & attributes

Rules that help ensure your test classes and methods are properly structured and decorated:

*   [MSTEST0002](mstest0002) - Test class should be valid
*   [MSTEST0003](mstest0003) - Test method should be valid
*   [MSTEST0004](mstest0004) - Public types should be test classes
*   [MSTEST0007](mstest0007) - Use attribute on test method
*   [MSTEST0016](mstest0016) - Test class should have test method
*   [MSTEST0029](mstest0029) - Public method should be test method
*   [MSTEST0030](mstest0030) - Type containing test method should be a test class
*   [MSTEST0036](mstest0036) - Do not use shadowing
*   [MSTEST0041](mstest0041) - Use condition-based attributes with test class
*   [MSTEST0044](mstest0044) - Prefer TestMethod over DataTestMethod
*   [MSTEST0056](mstest0056) - TestMethodAttribute should set DisplayName correctly
*   [MSTEST0057](mstest0057) - TestMethodAttribute should propagate source information
*   [MSTEST0060](mstest0060) - Duplicate TestMethodAttribute
*   [MSTEST0063](mstest0063) - Test class should have valid constructor

Related documentation: [Write tests with MSTest](../unit-testing-mstest-writing-tests)

### Async/await patterns

Rules for writing asynchronous test code correctly:

*   [MSTEST0013](mstest0013) - AssemblyCleanup should be valid (includes async rules)
*   [MSTEST0027](mstest0027) - Suppress async suffix for test methods
*   [MSTEST0028](mstest0028) - Suppress async suffix for test fixture methods
*   [MSTEST0039](mstest0039) - Use newer Assert.Throws methods (async variants)
*   [MSTEST0040](mstest0040) - Avoid using asserts in async void context
*   [MSTEST0045](mstest0045) - Use cooperative cancellation for timeout
*   [MSTEST0049](mstest0049) - Flow TestContext CancellationToken
*   [MSTEST0054](mstest0054) - Use CancellationToken property

Related documentation: [TestContext](../unit-testing-mstest-writing-tests-testcontext)

### Data-driven testing

Rules for working with data-driven test scenarios:

*   [MSTEST0007](mstest0007) - Use attribute on test method
*   [MSTEST0014](mstest0014) - DataRow should be valid
*   [MSTEST0018](mstest0018) - DynamicData should be valid
*   [MSTEST0042](mstest0042) - Duplicate DataRow
*   [MSTEST0052](mstest0052) - Avoid explicit DynamicDataSourceType
*   [MSTEST0062](mstest0062) - Avoid out/ref test method parameters

Related documentation: [Data-driven testing](../unit-testing-mstest-writing-tests-data-driven)

### Lifecycle & initialization

Rules for test initialization, cleanup, and lifecycle management:

*   [MSTEST0008](mstest0008) - TestInitialize should be valid
*   [MSTEST0009](mstest0009) - TestCleanup should be valid
*   [MSTEST0010](mstest0010) - ClassInitialize should be valid
*   [MSTEST0011](mstest0011) - ClassCleanup should be valid
*   [MSTEST0012](mstest0012) - AssemblyInitialize should be valid
*   [MSTEST0013](mstest0013) - AssemblyCleanup should be valid
*   [MSTEST0019](mstest0019) - Prefer TestInitialize over constructors
*   [MSTEST0020](mstest0020) - Prefer constructors over TestInitialize
*   [MSTEST0021](mstest0021) - Prefer Dispose over TestCleanup
*   [MSTEST0022](mstest0022) - Prefer TestCleanup over Dispose
*   [MSTEST0034](mstest0034) - Use ClassCleanupBehavior.EndOfClass
*   [MSTEST0050](mstest0050) - Global test fixture should be valid

Related documentation: [Lifecycle](../unit-testing-mstest-writing-tests-lifecycle)

### Assertions

Rules for using assertion methods correctly and effectively:

*   [MSTEST0006](mstest0006) - Avoid ExpectedException attribute
*   [MSTEST0014](mstest0014) - DataRow should be valid
*   [MSTEST0017](mstest0017) - Assertion args should be passed in correct order
*   [MSTEST0023](mstest0023) - Do not negate boolean assertion
*   [MSTEST0025](mstest0025) - Prefer Assert.Fail over always-false conditions
*   [MSTEST0026](mstest0026) - Assertion args should avoid conditional access
*   [MSTEST0032](mstest0032) - Review always-true assert condition
*   [MSTEST0037](mstest0037) - Use proper assert methods
*   [MSTEST0038](mstest0038) - Avoid Assert.AreSame with value types
*   [MSTEST0039](mstest0039) - Use newer Assert.Throws methods
*   [MSTEST0046](mstest0046) - Use Assert instead of StringAssert
*   [MSTEST0051](mstest0051) - Assert.Throws should contain single statement
*   [MSTEST0053](mstest0053) - Avoid Assert format parameters
*   [MSTEST0058](mstest0058) - Avoid asserts in catch blocks

Related documentation: [Assertions](../unit-testing-mstest-writing-tests-assertions)

### TestContext

Rules for properly using the TestContext object:

*   [MSTEST0005](mstest0005) - TestContext should be valid
*   [MSTEST0024](mstest0024) - Do not store static TestContext
*   [MSTEST0033](mstest0033) - Suppress non-nullable reference not initialized warning
*   [MSTEST0048](mstest0048) - TestContext property usage
*   [MSTEST0049](mstest0049) - Flow TestContext CancellationToken
*   [MSTEST0054](mstest0054) - Use CancellationToken property

Related documentation: [TestContext](../unit-testing-mstest-writing-tests-testcontext)

### Test configuration

Rules for configuring test execution, parallelization, and other test settings:

*   [MSTEST0001](mstest0001) - Use Parallelize attribute
*   [MSTEST0015](mstest0015) - Test method should not be ignored
*   [MSTEST0031](mstest0031) - Do not use System.ComponentModel.DescriptionAttribute
*   [MSTEST0035](mstest0035) - Use DeploymentItem with test method or test class
*   [MSTEST0043](mstest0043) - Use retry attribute on test method
*   [MSTEST0045](mstest0045) - Use cooperative cancellation for timeout
*   [MSTEST0055](mstest0055) - Do not ignore string method return value
*   [MSTEST0059](mstest0059) - Use Parallelize attribute correctly
*   [MSTEST0061](mstest0061) - Use OSCondition attribute instead of runtime check

Related documentation: [Configure MSTest](../unit-testing-mstest-configure), [Running tests](../unit-testing-mstest-running-tests)

## MSTESTEXP

Several APIs of MSTest are decorated with the [ExperimentalAttribute](/en-us/dotnet/api/system.diagnostics.codeanalysis.experimentalattribute). This attribute indicates that the API is experimental and may be removed or changed in future versions of MSTest. The attribute is used to identify APIs that aren't yet stable and may not be suitable for production use.

The MSTESTEXP diagnostic alerts you to use of an experimental API in your code. To suppress this diagnostic with the `SuppressMessageAttribute`, add the following code to your project:

```
using System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis;

[assembly: SuppressMessage("MSTESTEXP", "Justification")]
```

Alternatively, you can suppress this diagnostic with preprocessor directive by adding the following code to your project:

```
#pragma warning disable MSTESTEXP
        // API that is causing the warning.
#pragma warning restore MSTESTEXP
```

### TestContext

## MSTESTEXP

Several APIs of MSTest are decorated with the [ExperimentalAttribute](/en-us/dotnet/api/system.diagnostics.codeanalysis.experimentalattribute). This attribute indicates that the API is experimental and may be removed or changed in future versions of MSTest. The attribute is used to identify APIs that aren't yet stable and may not be suitable for production use.

The MSTESTEXP diagnostic alerts you to use of an experimental API in your code. To suppress this diagnostic with the `SuppressMessageAttribute`, add the following code to your project:

```
using System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis;

[assembly: SuppressMessage("MSTESTEXP", "Justification")]
```

Alternatively, you can suppress this diagnostic with preprocessor directive by adding the following code to your project:

```
#pragma warning disable MSTESTEXP
        // API that is causing the warning.
#pragma warning restore MSTESTEXP
```
result.detailedContent:
Contents of https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/core/testing/mstest-analyzers/overview:
_MSTest analysis_ ("MSTESTxxxx") rules inspect your C# or Visual Basic code for security, performance, design and other issues.

Tip

If you're using Visual Studio, many analyzer rules have associated _code fixes_ that you can apply to correct the problem. Code fixes are shown in the light bulb icon menu.

The rules are organized into categories such as performance usage...

Starting with MSTest.TestFramework 3.7, the MSTest.Analyzers NuGet package is a dependency of the framework. For earlier versions, you need to use `MSTest` metapackage or add a package reference for `MSTest.Analyzers` explicitly.

## MSTestAnalysisMode

Starting with MSTest 3.8, an MSBuild property named `MSTestAnalysisMode` is available to determine which analyzers are enabled at which severity.

Tip

To see which rules are enabled at which severity for each mode, you can navigate to the package of the version of interest in NuGet cache, locate the `globalconfigs` directory, and open the `.globalconfig` file corresponding to the analysis mode. For more information on finding the NuGet cache directory, see [Managing the global packages, cache, and temp folders](/en-us/nuget/consume-packages/managing-the-global-packages-and-cache-folders). From that directory, locate `mstest.analyzers` directory, then the version (3.8 and higher), then `globalconfigs`. Alternatively, you can download the NuGet package of the version of interest from `nuget.org` and view it in NuGet Package Explorer (Windows app), or view directly in the [web app version of NuGet Package Explorer](https://nuget.info/packages/MSTest.Analyzers/).

The available values for this property:

*   [`None`](#none)
*   [`Default`](#default)
*   [`Recommended`](#recommended)
*   [`All`](#all)

### `None`

This value sets all analyzers to `none` severity, disabling all of them. You can then enable individual analyzers using `.editorconfig` or `.globalconfig` files.

### `Default`

This setting follows the default documented behavior for each rule.

*   Rules that are enabled by default will use their default severity.
*   Rules that are disabled by default will use `none` severity.

Note

Rules that are enabled by default as warnings are violations that are expected to cause issues at runtime.

### `Recommended`

This is the mode most developers are expected to use. Rules that are enabled by default with Info (`suggestion`) severity are escalated to warnings. The following rules are escalated to errors in both `Recommended` and `All` modes:

*   [MSTEST0003: Test methods should have valid layout](mstest0003).
*   [MSTEST0043: Use retry attribute on test method](mstest0043).

### `All`

This mode is more aggressive than `Recommended`. All rules are enabled as warnings. In addition, the following rules are escalated to errors:

*   [MSTEST0003: Test methods should have valid layout](mstest0003).
*   [MSTEST0043: Use retry attribute on test method](mstest0043).

## Rules by category

The analyzer rules are organized into the following categories:

### Design rules

[Design rules](design-rules) help you create and maintain test suites that adhere to proper design and good practices.

### Performance rules

[Performance rules](performance-rules) support high-performance testing.

### Suppression rules

[Suppression rules](suppression-rules) support suppressing diagnostics from other rules.

### Usage rules

[Usage rules](usage-rules) support proper usage of MSTest.

## Rules by concept

Find rules organized by common testing scenarios and concepts:

### Test structure & attributes

Rules that help ensure your test classes and methods are properly structured and decorated:

*   [MSTEST0002](mstest0002) - Test class should be valid
*   [MSTEST0003](mstest0003) - Test method should be valid
*   [MSTEST0004](mstest0004) - Public types should be test classes
*   [MSTEST0007](mstest0007) - Use attribute on test method
*   [MSTEST0016](mstest0016) - Test class should have test method
*   [MSTEST0029](mstest0029) - Public method should be test method
*   [MSTEST0030](mstest0030) - Type containing test method should be a test class
*   [MSTEST0036](mstest0036) - Do not use shadowing
*   [MSTEST0041](mstest0041) - Use condition-based attributes with test class
*   [MSTEST0044](mstest0044) - Prefer TestMethod over DataTestMethod
*   [MSTEST0056](mstest0056) - TestMethodAttribute should set DisplayName correctly
*   [MSTEST0057](mstest0057) - TestMethodAttribute should propagate source information
*   [MSTEST0060](mstest0060) - Duplicate TestMethodAttribute
*   [MSTEST0063](mstest0063) - Test class should have valid constructor

Related documentation: [Write tests with MSTest](../unit-testing-mstest-writing-tests)

### Async/await patterns

Rules for writing asynchronous test code correctly:

*   [MSTEST0013](mstest0013) - AssemblyCleanup should be valid (includes async rules)
*   [MSTEST0027](mstest0027) - Suppress async suffix for test methods
*   [MSTEST0028](mstest0028) - Suppress async suffix for test fixture methods
*   [MSTEST0039](mstest0039) - Use newer Assert.Throws methods (async variants)
*   [MSTEST0040](mstest0040) - Avoid using asserts in async void context
*   [MSTEST0045](mstest0045) - Use cooperative cancellation for timeout
*   [MSTEST0049](mstest0049) - Flow TestContext CancellationToken
*   [MSTEST0054](mstest0054) - Use CancellationToken property

Related documentation: [TestContext](../unit-testing-mstest-writing-tests-testcontext)

### Data-driven testing

Rules for working with data-driven test scenarios:

*   [MSTEST0007](mstest0007) - Use attribute on test method
*   [MSTEST0014](mstest0014) - DataRow should be valid
*   [MSTEST0018](mstest0018) - DynamicData should be valid
*   [MSTEST0042](mstest0042) - Duplicate DataRow
*   [MSTEST0052](mstest0052) - Avoid explicit DynamicDataSourceType
*   [MSTEST0062](mstest0062) - Avoid out/ref test method parameters

Related documentation: [Data-driven testing](../unit-testing-mstest-writing-tests-data-driven)

### Lifecycle & initialization

Rules for test initialization, cleanup, and lifecycle management:

*   [MSTEST0008](mstest0008) - TestInitialize should be valid
*   [MSTEST0009](mstest0009) - TestCleanup should be valid
*   [MSTEST0010](mstest0010) - ClassInitialize should be valid
*   [MSTEST0011](mstest0011) - ClassCleanup should be valid
*   [MSTEST0012](mstest0012) - AssemblyInitialize should be valid
*   [MSTEST0013](mstest0013) - AssemblyCleanup should be valid
*   [MSTEST0019](mstest0019) - Prefer TestInitialize over constructors
*   [MSTEST0020](mstest0020) - Prefer constructors over TestInitialize
*   [MSTEST0021](mstest0021) - Prefer Dispose over TestCleanup
*   [MSTEST0022](mstest0022) - Prefer TestCleanup over Dispose
*   [MSTEST0034](mstest0034) - Use ClassCleanupBehavior.EndOfClass
*   [MSTEST0050](mstest0050) - Global test fixture should be valid

Related documentation: [Lifecycle](../unit-testing-mstest-writing-tests-lifecycle)

### Assertions

Rules for using assertion methods correctly and effectively:

*   [MSTEST0006](mstest0006) - Avoid ExpectedException attribute
*   [MSTEST0014](mstest0014) - DataRow should be valid
*   [MSTEST0017](mstest0017) - Assertion args should be passed in correct order
*   [MSTEST0023](mstest0023) - Do not negate boolean assertion
*   [MSTEST0025](mstest0025) - Prefer Assert.Fail over always-false conditions
*   [MSTEST0026](mstest0026) - Assertion args should avoid conditional access
*   [MSTEST0032](mstest0032) - Review always-true assert condition
*   [MSTEST0037](mstest0037) - Use proper assert methods
*   [MSTEST0038](mstest0038) - Avoid Assert.AreSame with value types
*   [MSTEST0039](mstest0039) - Use newer Assert.Throws methods
*   [MSTEST0046](mstest0046) - Use Assert instead of StringAssert
*   [MSTEST0051](mstest0051) - Assert.Throws should contain single statement
*   [MSTEST0053](mstest0053) - Avoid Assert format parameters
*   [MSTEST0058](mstest0058) - Avoid asserts in catch blocks

Related documentation: [Assertions](../unit-testing-mstest-writing-tests-assertions)

### TestContext

Rules for properly using the TestContext object:

*   [MSTEST0005](mstest0005) - TestContext should be valid
*   [MSTEST0024](mstest0024) - Do not store static TestContext
*   [MSTEST0033](mstest0033) - Suppress non-nullable reference not initialized warning
*   [MSTEST0048](mstest0048) - TestContext property usage
*   [MSTEST0049](mstest0049) - Flow TestContext CancellationToken
*   [MSTEST0054](mstest0054) - Use CancellationToken property

Related documentation: [TestContext](../unit-testing-mstest-writing-tests-testcontext)

### Test configuration

Rules for configuring test execution, parallelization, and other test settings:

*   [MSTEST0001](mstest0001) - Use Parallelize attribute
*   [MSTEST0015](mstest0015) - Test method should not be ignored
*   [MSTEST0031](mstest0031) - Do not use System.ComponentModel.DescriptionAttribute
*   [MSTEST0035](mstest0035) - Use DeploymentItem with test method or test class
*   [MSTEST0043](mstest0043) - Use retry attribute on test method
*   [MSTEST0045](mstest0045) - Use cooperative cancellation for timeout
*   [MSTEST0055](mstest0055) - Do not ignore string method return value
*   [MSTEST0059](mstest0059) - Use Parallelize attribute correctly
*   [MSTEST0061](mstest0061) - Use OSCondition attribute instead of runtime check

Related documentation: [Configure MSTest](../unit-testing-mstest-configure), [Running tests](../unit-testing-mstest-running-tests)

## MSTESTEXP

Several APIs of MSTest are decorated with the [ExperimentalAttribute](/en-us/dotnet/api/system.diagnostics.codeanalysis.experimentalattribute). This attribute indicates that the API is experimental and may be removed or changed in future versions of MSTest. The attribute is used to identify APIs that aren't yet stable and may not be suitable for production use.

The MSTESTEXP diagnostic alerts you to use of an experimental API in your code. To suppress this diagnostic with the `SuppressMessageAttribute`, add the following code to your project:

```
using System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis;

[assembly: SuppressMessage("MSTESTEXP", "Justification")]
```

Alternatively, you can suppress this diagnostic with preprocessor directive by adding the following code to your project:

```
#pragma warning disable MSTESTEXP
        // API that is causing the warning.
#pragma warning restore MSTESTEXP
```

Description

[AspNetDevelopmentServerPrefix](microsoft.visualstudio.testtools.unittesting.testcontext.aspnetdevelopmentserverprefix?view=mstest-net-uap-4.1#microsoft-visualstudio-testtools-unittesting-testcontext-aspnetdevelopmentserverprefix)

## Properties

Name

Description

[CancellationToken](microsoft.visualstudio.testtools.unittesting.testcontext.cancellationtoken?view=mstest-net-uap-4.1#microsoft-visualstudio-testtools-unittesting-testcontext-cancellationtoken)

Gets the cancellation token. This token is canceled when test times out. Also when explicitly canceled the test will be aborted.

[CancellationTokenSource](microsoft.visualstudio.testtools.unittesting.testcontext.cancellationtokensource?view=mstest-net-uap-4.1#microsoft-visualstudio-testtools-unittesting-testcontext-cancellationtokensource)

Gets or sets the cancellation token source. This token source is canceled when test times out. Also when explicitly canceled the test will be aborted

[CurrentTestOutcome](microsoft.visualstudio.testtools.unittesting.testcontext.currenttestoutcome?view=mstest-net-uap-4.1#microsoft-visualstudio-testtools-unittesting-testcontext-currenttestoutcome)

Gets the current test outcome.

[DataConnection](microsoft.visualstudio.testtools.unittesting.testcontext.dataconnection?view=mstest-net-uap-4.1#microsoft-visualstudio-testtools-unittesting-testcontext-dataconnection)

Gets current data connection row when test is used for data driven testing.

[DataRow](microsoft.visualstudio.testtools.unittesting.testcontext.datarow?view=mstest-net-uap-4.1#microsoft-visualstudio-testtools-unittesting-testcontext-datarow)

Gets the current data row when test is used for data driven testing.

[DeploymentDirectory](microsoft.visualstudio.testtools.unittesting.testcontext.deploymentdirectory?view=mstest-net-uap-4.1#microsoft-visualstudio-testtools-unittesting-testcontext-deploymentdirectory)

Gets directory for files deployed for the test run. Typically a subdirectory of [TestRunDirectory](microsoft.visualstudio.testtools.unittesting.testcontext.testrundirectory?view=mstest-net-uap-4.1#microsoft-visualstudio-testtools-unittesting-testcontext-testrundirectory).

[FullyQualifiedTestClassName](microsoft.visualstudio.testtools.unittesting.testcontext.fullyqualifiedtestclassname?view=mstest-net-uap-4.1#microsoft-visualstudio-testtools-unittesting-testcontext-fullyqualifiedtestclassname)

Gets the Fully-qualified name of the class containing the test method currently being executed

[ManagedMethod](microsoft.visualstudio.testtools.unittesting.testcontext.managedmethod?view=mstest-net-uap-4.1#microsoft-visualstudio-testtools-unittesting-testcontext-managedmethod)

Gets the fully specified method name metadata format.

[ManagedType](microsoft.visualstudio.testtools.unittesting.testcontext.managedtype?view=mstest-net-uap-4.1#microsoft-visualstudio-testtools-unittesting-testcontext-managedtype)

Gets the fully specified type name metadata format.

[Properties](microsoft.visualstudio.testtools.unittesting.testcontext.properties?view=mstest-net-uap-4.1#microsoft-visualstudio-testtools-unittesting-testcontext-properties)

Gets test properties for a test.

[RequestedPage](microsoft.visualstudio.testtools.unittesting.testcontext.requestedpage?view=mstest-net-uap-4.1#microsoft-visualstudio-testtools-unittesting-testcontext-requestedpage)

[ResultsDirectory](microsoft.visualstudio.testtools.unittesting.testcontext.resultsdirectory?view=mstest-net-uap-4.1#microsoft-visualstudio-testtools-unittesting-testcontext-resultsdirectory)

Gets base directory for results from the test run. Typically a subdirectory of [TestRunDirectory](microsoft.visualstudio.testtools.unittesting.testcontext.testrundirectory?view=mstest-net-uap-4.1#microsoft-visualstudio-testtools-unittesting-testcontext-testrundirectory).

[TestData](microsoft.visualstudio.testtools.unittesting.testcontext.testdata?view=mstest-net-uap-4.1#microsoft-visualstudio-testtools-unittesting-testcontext-testdata)

Gets or sets the test data for the test method being executed.

[TestDeploymentDir](microsoft.visualstudio.testtools.unittesting.testcontext.testdeploymentdir?view=mstest-net-uap-4.1#microsoft-visualstudio-testtools-unittesting-testcontext-testdeploymentdir)

**Obsolete.**

Gets directory for files deployed for the test run. Typically a subdirectory of [TestRunDirectory](microsoft.visualstudio.testtools.unittesting.testcontext.testrundirectory?view=mstest-net-uap-4.1#microsoft-visualstudio-testtools-unittesting-testcontext-testrundirectory). Same as [DeploymentDirectory](microsoft.visualstudio.testtools.unittesting.testcontext.deploymentdirectory?view=mstest-net-uap-4.1#microsoft-visualstudio-testtools-unittesting-testcontext-deploymentdirectory). Use that property instead.

[TestDir](microsoft.visualstudio.testtools.unittesting.testcontext.testdir?view=mstest-net-uap-4.1#microsoft-visualstudio-testtools-unittesting-testcontext-testdir)

**Obsolete.**

Gets base directory for the test run, under which deployed files and result files are stored. Same as [TestRunDirectory](microsoft.visualstudio.testtools.unittesting.testcontext.testrundirectory?view=mstest-net-uap-4.1#microsoft-visualstudio-testtools-unittesting-testcontext-testrundirectory). Use that property instead.

[TestDisplayName](microsoft.visualstudio.testtools.unittesting.testcontext.testdisplayname?view=mstest-net-uap-4.1#microsoft-visualstudio-testtools-unittesting-testcontext-testdisplayname)

Gets or sets the test display name for the test method being executed.

[TestException](microsoft.visualstudio.testtools.unittesting.testcontext.testexception?view=mstest-net-uap-4.1#microsoft-visualstudio-testtools-unittesting-testcontext-testexception)

Gets or sets the exception that occurred in the TestInitialize or TestMethod steps.

[TestLogsDir](microsoft.visualstudio.testtools.unittesting.testcontext.testlogsdir?view=mstest-net-uap-4.1#microsoft-visualstudio-testtools-unittesting-testcontext-testlogsdir)

**Obsolete.**

Gets directory for test run result files. Typically a subdirectory of [ResultsDirectory](microsoft.visualstudio.testtools.unittesting.testcontext.resultsdirectory?view=mstest-net-uap-4.1#microsoft-visualstudio-testtools-unittesting-testcontext-resultsdirectory). Same as [TestRunResultsDirectory](microsoft.visualstudio.testtools.unittesting.testcontext.testrunresultsdirectory?view=mstest-net-uap-4.1#microsoft-visualstudio-testtools-unittesting-testcontext-testrunresultsdirectory). Use that property for test run result files, or [TestResultsDirectory](microsoft.visualstudio.testtools.unittesting.testcontext.testresultsdirectory?view=mstest-net-uap-4.1#microsoft-visualstudio-testtools-unittesting-testcontext-testresultsdirectory) for test-specific result files instead.

[TestName](microsoft.visualstudio.testtools.unittesting.testcontext.testname?view=mstest-net-uap-4.1#microsoft-visualstudio-testtools-unittesting-testcontext-testname)

Gets the name of the test method currently being executed

[TestResultsDirectory](microsoft.visualstudio.testtools.unittesting.testcontext.testresultsdirectory?view=mstest-net-uap-4.1#microsoft-visualstudio-testtools-unittesting-testcontext-testresultsdirectory)

Gets directory for test result files.

[TestRunCount](microsoft.visualstudio.testtools.unittesting.testcontext.testruncount?view=mstest-net-uap-4.1#microsoft-visualstudio-testtools-unittesting-testcontext-testruncount)

Gets the current attempt of the test run. This property is relevant when using [RetryAttribute](microsoft.visualstudio.testtools.unittesting.retryattribute?view=mstest-net-uap-4.1) (or any implementation of [RetryBaseAttribute](microsoft.visualstudio.testtools.unittesting.retrybaseattribute?view=mstest-net-uap-4.1)). On the first run, this property is set to 1. On subsequent retries, the value is incremented.

[TestRunDirectory](microsoft.visualstudio.testtools.unittesting.testcontext.testrundirectory?view=mstest-net-uap-4.1#microsoft-visualstudio-testtools-unittesting-testcontext-testrundirectory)

Gets base directory for the test run, under which deployed files and result files are stored.

[TestRunResultsDirectory](microsoft.visualstudio.testtools.unittesting.testcontext.testrunresultsdirectory?view=mstest-net-uap-4.1#microsoft-visualstudio-testtools-unittesting-testcontext-testrunresultsdirectory)

Gets directory for test run result files. Typically a subdirectory of [ResultsDirectory](microsoft.visualstudio.testtools.unittesting.testcontext.resultsdirectory?view=mstest-net-uap-4.1#microsoft-visualstudio-testtools-unittesting-testcontext-resultsdirectory).

data.content:
Now I have all the facts. Let me fix the code:

1. **`ManagedType`** — removed in v4; use `FullyQualifiedTestClassName` instead
2. **`TestTimeout`** — enum removed in v4; use `int.MaxValue` directly
3. **`Properties.Contains()`** — `Properties` changed from `IDictionary` to `IDictionary<string, object?>`; use `ContainsKey()` instead
data.reasoningText:
Now I have all the information I need:

1. **`TestContext.ManagedType`** - Removed in v4. The property `ManagedType` and `ManagedMethod` were removed from `TestContext`. Use `TestContext.FullyQualifiedTestClassName` instead (which is still there).

2. **`TestTimeout`** - The `TestTimeout` enum was completely removed in v4 (it was marked obsolete in v3 with the message: "The 'TestTimeout' attribute is obsolete and will be removed in v4. Consider removing the 'Timeout' attribute or use the 'Timeout' attribute with the 'int.MaxValue' for infinite timeout."). Replace `TestTimeout.Infinite` with `int.MaxValue`.


3. **`Properties.Contains`** - The `Properties` collection changed from a non-generic `IDictionary` to `IDictionary<string, object?>`, so I need to swap out `Contains()` calls for `ContainsKey()` instead.

Now I'm ready to update the code.

data.content:
Build succeeds. Here's a summary of the three fixes and additional v4 changes to know about:

### Fixes applied

| Error | Cause | Fix |
|-------|-------|-----|
| `TestContext.ManagedType` (CS1061) | Property removed in v4 | → `TestContext.FullyQualifiedTestClassName` |
| `TestTimeout.Infinite` (CS0103) | `TestTimeout` enum removed in v4 | → `int.MaxValue` |
| `Properties.Contains()` (CS1061) | `Properties` changed from `IDictionary` to `IDictionary<string, object?>` | → `Properties.ContainsKey()` |

### Other MSTest v4 behavior changes to know

- **MTP runner is default** — MSTest.Sdk v4 uses the Microsoft Testing Platform runner (not VSTest) by default. This changes how `dotnet test` works and may require CI updates (add `-- --report-trx --coverage` flags). Set `<UseVSTest>true</UseVSTest>` to opt out.
- **`ManagedMethod` also removed** — if you used it elsewhere, use `TestContext.TestName` instead.
- **Stricter analyzers** — v4 ships with `MSTestAnalysisMode` defaulting to `Default`, which enables more warnings (e.g., MSTEST0032 above). You can configure this via `<MSTestAnalysisMode>` in your csproj.
- **`TestContext` via constructor** — v3.6+ supports injecting `TestContext` through the constructor (preferred over the property setter pattern).
